texto: The older I get, the more I see there are these crevices in life where things fall in and you just can't reach them to pull them back out. So you can sit next to them and weep or you can get up and move forward. You have to stop worrying about who's not here and start worrying about who is.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Alex Witchel sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “The older I get, the more I see there are these crevices in life where things fall in and you jus…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, aging, sadness, útil em momentos de sadness.

frase_original: The older I get, the more I see there are these crevices in life where things fall in and you just can't reach them to pull them back out. So you can sit next to them and weep or you can get up and move forward. You have to stop worrying about who's not here and start worrying about who is.
